incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 1—would create 32 tick marks for axis range 0-31

params.description: Vertical bar graph appears with Scale of 1. Points Scored data. Axis 0-31 showing all 32 tick marks. All bars land exactly on marks.

steps.text: Scale of 1 does show exact values. But count the tick marks from 0 to 31. That's a lot of lines on the axis. Which scale would be exact and easier to read?

incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 5—cannot show 22, 8, or 31 exactly since they are not multiples of 5

params.description: Vertical bar graph appears with Scale of 5. Points Scored data. Warning indicators on Round 1 (22), Round 3 (8), and Round 4 (31) bars.

steps.text: Look at the ones digits again: 2, 5, 8, 1. Three values don't end in 0 or 5, so they won't land exactly on a Scale of 5. Which scale shows all whole numbers exactly?

incorrects.description: Student selected Scale of 10—cannot show 22, 15, 8, or 31 exactly since they are not multiples of 10

params.description: Vertical bar graph appears with Scale of 10. Points Scored data. Warning indicators on all four bars—none land exactly on tick marks or midpoints.

steps.text: None of these values end in 0 or 5. On Scale of 10, you can't tell where the bars end exactly. Which scale lets you show any whole number precisely?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly three or more times. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Vertical bar graph appears with Scale of 2. Points Scored data. All four bars land exactly on tick marks or midpoints.

steps.text: Let me show you how to choose the right scale. First, I check the ones digits: 2, 5, 8, 1. Three values don't end in 0 or 5, so Scales of 5 and 10 won't show them exactly. Scale of 1 would work, but 31 tick marks is too many. Scale of 2 is the answer. With Scale of 2, half of 2 is 1, so any whole number lands exactly on a line or halfway between lines. Round 1 is 22, right on the 22 mark. Round 2 is 15, halfway between 14 and 16. Round 3 is 8, right on the 8 mark. Round 4 is 31, halfway between 30 and 32. The answer is Scale of 2 because it shows all values exactly without overcrowding the axis.
